sections.heading: Prognosis
sections.content: Kidney cancer prognosis varies significantly by stage and histology. Approximately 66% of cases are diagnosed as localized disease, with an overall 5-year survival rate of 79% (2015-2021 data). The most important prognostic determinants of 5-year survival are tumor stage, grade, local extent of the tumor, presence of regional nodal metastases, and evidence of metastatic disease at presentation. RCC primarily metastasizes to the lung, bone, liver, lymph nodes, adrenal gland, and brain.
